Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Scenario Step-by-Step Step 1 Arrival and Intake Activity A Activity RG Activity IA Relationship Sequential Tree Fragment -> ( A, -> ( RG, IA ) ) or -> ( A, RG, IA ) depending on binary operator definition The prompt implies binary operators -> (A, B) So -> ( A, -> ( RG, IA ) ) Step 2 Triage Activities TI TC Relationship Sequential Relationship to Step 1 Sequential Tree Fragment -> ( Step1, -> ( TI, TC ) )

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Step 3 Initial Diagnostics Exclusive Choice Activities CE or FBP Relationship Exclusive Choice X Relationship to Step 2 Sequential Tree Fragment -> ( Step2, X ( CE, FBP ) )

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Step 4 The Loop Structure 1 Enter Loop 2 SC Specialist Consultation 3 X Treatment Choice TM or SPT 4 RDT Re-Diagnostic Test 5 Inside RDT exclusive choice X ( FCE, FBA ) 6 Loop Condition If abnormal repeat If normal exit

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Correction on Loop Definition The prompt defines * ( A, B ) as A is executed then either you exit the loop or you execute B and then A again So * ( Body, Continue ) Body SC then X(TM, SPT) then RDT Continue the tricky part

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Interpretation 1 A main body B condition to continue No Standard PM4Py Loop operator usually takes body and continue block Prompt explicitly defines * ( A, B ) Parse the prompt definition strictly A executed then choice Exit OR Execute B then A This implies B represents the Yes keep going branch Exit is the No branch Usually in process trees the loop handles repetition If B executed go back to A If not exit

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Map the specific logic Loop Body A SC -> X(TM, SPT) -> RDT where RDT is X(FCE, FBA) Continue Condition B Abnormal result path Exit Condition Normal result path The RDT is the check If RDT results abnormal B triggers re-iteration B tau silent step representing Abnormal continue

Name: Backtracking - NE
Text: RDT modeling issue Text RDT itself is an exclusive choice Either FCE or FBA Expected list includes RDT FCE FBA Contradiction Step 3 CE or FBP list CE FBP no Initial Test Various options -> ( RDT, X(FCE, FBA) ) trace RDT FCE X(FCE, FBA) trace FCE or FBA RDT as parent label no leaves only Treat RDT as XOR no label on internal Prioritize text structure X(FCE, FBA) or include RDT before Many alternatives considered decision X(FCE, FBA)

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Step 5 Advanced Diagnostics Concurrency AI NC True Concurrency + Relationship to Step 4 Sequential Tree Fragment -> ( Step4, + ( AI, NC ) ) Step 6 Final Admin Sequence FAS DDI Sequential -> ( Step5, -> ( FAS, DDI ) ) Step 7 Final Clearances Concurrency BL ICP MRF True Concurrency + nested for three + ( BL, + ( ICP, MRF ) )

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Refining the Construction Code Implementation Use tau for loop continuation B tau silent Nested + for three parallel Explicit parent setting Handle binary operators by nesting Self-Correction on Loop * ( Body, tau ) Choice Exit or tau + Body trace Body repeated fits requirement

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Review of the Plan Self-Correction on Loop Body -> ( SC, -> ( X(TM, SPT), X(FCE, FBA) ) ) Continue tau Final check on Parallel + ( BL, + ( ICP, MRF ) ) Sequence nested XORs Leafs activities or tau Parent pointers Note on pm4py constructor
